flavor_text: Twine knotted around a chipped token from a roadside shrine.
Carved from old bone and polished with lamp oil.
Its hinge complains like a rat trapped in stone.
A pocket charm hung at the throat for cheap courage.
No crest, no blessing, only weight against the sternum.
Forged for caravan guards who sleep with boots on.
A duty medal darkened by rain and old ash.
The cord smells faintly of pyres and wet wool.
Inside rests a map scrap to a village already burned.
Linked steel for those who patrol plague fences at night.
Thin runes crawl along its face when moonlight thins.
Pale metal that glows like frost before a battle.
Stormglass trapped in iron, humming when skies go black.
Pinned once to a knight who never returned from the fen.
Its crescent edge is worn smooth by sleepless thumbs.
Priests called it blessed, gravediggers called it warning.
A royal locket emptied before the palace doors were barred.
Awarded after sieges that left more crows than citizens.
Gilded rings frame a core of soot-streaked bone.
The stone inside shines brightest where shadows are deepest.
Each silver thorn marks a debt still unpaid.
Heavy links cast for ceremonies of allegiance and fear.
Cold at noon, warm at midnight, never for good reasons.
It catches sunrise like blood on polished steel.
A throne relic whispered to outlast dynasties and mercy.
